name: Docker Publish
description: Build, tag, and push a Docker image to a container registry

inputs.name: app-name
inputs.description: Application name; used in image tags

inputs.name: registry
inputs.description: Container registry URL (e.g. ghcr.io, docker.io, aws_account_id.dkr.ecr.region.amazonaws.com)

inputs.name: dockerfile
inputs.description: Path to the Dockerfile

inputs.name: build-context
inputs.description: Docker build context directory

inputs.name: build-task
inputs.description: mise task for pre-build steps (optional)

inputs.name: additional-tags
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of additional tags to apply

name: GitHub Release Build
description: Build the application and upload as artifact for release

inputs.name: app-name
inputs.description: Application name; used in artifact names

inputs.name: build-task
inputs.description: mise task that builds the application

inputs.name: build-output
inputs.description: Directory containing the built output

name: GitHub Release Publish
description: Download the build artifact and create a GitHub Release

inputs.name: app-name
inputs.description: Application name; used to download the correct artifact

inputs.name: build-output
inputs.description: Directory where the built artifact was extracted

name: GitHub Pages Publish
description: Build a static site and deploy it to GitHub Pages

inputs.name: build-command
inputs.description: Command to build the static site (e.g. bun run build, npm run build, make build)

inputs.name: output-directory
inputs.description: Directory containing the built static files (relative to repo root)

inputs.name: setup-command
inputs.description: Optional command to run before the build (e.g. npm ci, bun install, pip install -r requirements.txt)

inputs.name: base-path
inputs.description: Base path for the site. For user/org sites (username.github.io) this is empty. For project sites (username.github.io/repo) this is /repo. Leave empty to let the action derive it from the repository name.

name: PyPI Build
description: Build a Python package wheel and upload it as an artifact

inputs.name: app-name
inputs.description: Application name; used in artifact names

inputs.name: pypi-package-dir
inputs.description: Directory containing built artifacts (relative to repo root)

inputs.name: build-task
inputs.description: mise task that builds the package wheel

name: Python Publish
description: Publish a built Python package to PyPI (Trusted Publishing), GitHub Packages, or JFrog Artifactory.

inputs.name: app-name
inputs.description: Application name; used in artifact names

inputs.name: registry
inputs.description: Target registry (pypi, github, jfrog)

inputs.name: packages-dir
inputs.description: Directory containing built artifacts

inputs.name: jfrog-url
inputs.description: JFrog Artifactory PyPI repository URL (required when registry=jfrog)

inputs.name: jfrog-user
inputs.description: JFrog username (required when registry=jfrog)

inputs.name: jfrog-token
inputs.description: JFrog API token or password (required when registry=jfrog)

name: Drift Check
description: Run blueprint check on a PR and post a comment if drift is detected. Automatically removes the comment when drift is resolved.

inputs.name: blueprint-file
inputs.description: Path to the .bp file inside the repo (e.g. setup.bp)

inputs.name: template
inputs.description: Template path or @github shorthand (e.g. @github:elpic/templates@main:containers/python)

inputs.name: against
inputs.description: Directory or file to check against (e.g. . or src/)

name: Go Build
description: Build the Go binary and upload it as an artifact

inputs.name: setup
inputs.description: Tool setup method (bash, mise, node, just, none)

inputs.name: node-version
inputs.description: Node.js version (used when setup=node)

inputs.name: build-task
inputs.description: Task to run

name: Go Integration Tests
description: Download the built artifact and run integration tests

inputs.name: setup
inputs.description: Tool setup method (bash, mise, node, just, none)

inputs.name: node-version
inputs.description: Node.js version (used when setup=node)

inputs.name: app-name
inputs.description: Application name; used in artifact names

inputs.name: integration-task
inputs.description: Task to run

inputs.name: env
inputs.description: Environment variables to pass to the test process, one KEY=VALUE per line. Example: DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:test@localhost:5432/test

name: Go Lint
description: Run the Go linter via mise

inputs.name: setup
inputs.description: Tool setup method (bash, mise, node, just, none)

inputs.name: node-version
inputs.description: Node.js version (used when setup=node)

inputs.name: lint-task
inputs.description: Task to run

description: Run security scans via mise

inputs.name: setup
inputs.description: Tool setup method (bash, mise, node, just, none)

inputs.name: node-version
inputs.description: Node.js version (used when setup=node)

inputs.name: security-task
inputs.description: Task to run

name: Go Test
description: Run tests with coverage comparison against the main branch

inputs.name: setup
inputs.description: Tool setup method (bash, mise, node, just, none)

inputs.name: node-version
inputs.description: Node.js version (used when setup=node)

inputs.name: test-coverage-task
inputs.description: Task that runs tests and produces coverage.out

inputs.name: timeout-minutes
inputs.description: Step timeout in minutes

description: Run tests with coverage comparison against the main branch

inputs.name: test-coverage-task

inputs.description: Task to run

inputs.name: env
inputs.description: Environment variables to pass to the test process, one KEY=VALUE per line. Example: DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:test@localhost:5432/test

description: Run security scans via mise

name: Python Test
description: Run tests with coverage comparison against the main branch

inputs.name: setup
inputs.description: Tool setup method (bash, mise, node, just, none)

inputs.name: node-version
inputs.description: Node.js version (used when setup=node)

inputs.name: test-coverage-task
inputs.description: Task that runs pytest and produces coverage.xml

inputs.name: test-task
inputs.description: Task that runs tests without coverage

inputs.name: timeout-minutes
inputs.description: Step timeout in minutes

name: Mirror Docker Image
description: Mirror a Docker image from a source registry (e.g. Docker Hub) to a mirror registry (default: GHCR), avoiding rate limits by reusing the mirrored copy on later runs.

inputs.name: source
inputs.description: Source image reference including tag (e.g. mockoon/cli:9.6.1, postgres:16). If no tag is provided, :latest is assumed.

inputs.name: target
inputs.description: Target image reference in the mirror registry. If not provided, one is auto-generated from the source and repository owner.

inputs.name: github_token
inputs.description: GitHub token with packages:write permission to authenticate with the GitHub Container Registry.

inputs.name: dockerhub_username
inputs.description: Docker Hub username for authenticated pulls (optional). Authenticated pulls have a higher rate limit.

inputs.name: dockerhub_token
inputs.description: Docker Hub token or password for authenticated pulls.

inputs.name: force
inputs.description: If true, always pull from the source and re-push to the mirror, even if the image already exists. Useful for mutable tags like :latest.

name: Generate OCI Tags
description: Generate OCI-compliant Docker image tags (semver, commit SHA, branch, latest) in a format ready for docker/build-push-action or docker tag + push. Supports semver version parsing, branch sanitization, and pre-release detection.

inputs.name: registry
inputs.description: Container registry URL (e.g. ghcr.io, docker.io)

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Image name (e.g. myapp, org/myapp)

inputs.name: version
inputs.description: Semver version string (e.g. v1.2.3 or 1.2.3). Generates tags for the full version, major.minor, and major. Pre-release versions (e.g. v2.0.0-rc.1) skip the latest tag per Docker convention.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: Full commit SHA

inputs.name: sha-length
inputs.description: Number of characters to use for the short SHA tag

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Git branch or tag name

inputs.name: additional-tags
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of extra tags to append

inputs.name: include-latest
inputs.description: Include the latest tag (automatically skipped for pre-releases)

name: Setup Just
description: Install the just command runner (https://just.systems)

name: Setup mise
description: Checkout code and install mise (https://mise.jdx.dev)

inputs.name: fetch-depth
inputs.description: Number of commits to fetch. 0 indicates all history (needed for blame, diff, and comparing against the base branch).

name: Update Major Tag
description: After tagging a new semver release (e.g. v1.2.3), update the major (v1) and minor (v1.2) floating tags so users pinning to @v1 always get the latest v1.x release.

inputs.name: tag
inputs.description: The semver tag that was just created (e.g. v1.2.3). Must already exist.

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: GitHub token with permission to push tags

inputs.name: update-minor
inputs.description: Whether to also update the minor tag (e.g. v1.2)

name: Upsert PR Comment
description: Post a new PR comment, update an existing one (matched by prefix), or delete it.

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: GitHub token with permission to write PR comments

inputs.name: comment-prefix
inputs.description: The prefix string that identifies the comment (e.g. "## Coverage Report"). Used to find and update the existing comment instead of creating duplicates.

inputs.name: comment-body
inputs.description: The full markdown body of the comment.

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Operation to perform. - upsert (default): create or update a comment matching the prefix - delete: remove the comment matching the prefix
